title: Assyrian merchant caravan — Aššur to Kaneš

intro.title: Tin and Textiles up the Tigris
intro.body: The Old Assyrian merchant archive of Kaneš (Kültepe) documents ~22,000 tablets of caravan trade between Aššur and central Anatolia. Caravans of black donkeys carried tin and woven textiles north, returning with silver. The round trip took about six weeks.

stops.name: Aššur
stops.note: Old Assyrian capital on the Tigris. Origin of the caravans; tin arrived here from the east before being repackaged on donkey-back.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mud-brick citadel above the Tigris, lines of donkeys laden with cloth-wrapped tin ingots leaving the gate.

stops.name: Apum (Tell Leilan)
stops.note: Stopping-place in the Khabur triangle. Tablets from Tell Leilan attest its role as caravan node in the period of Shamshi-Adad.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Walled tell on a steppe, caravanserai courtyard with donkeys at troughs.

stops.name: Hahhum
stops.note: Toll-station at a Euphrates crossing on the route to Anatolia. Identified tentatively with Lidar Höyük.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. River crossing with reed-bundle rafts ferrying donkeys, officials with clay tablets at a customs post.

stops.name: Mamma
stops.note: Intermediate town in the Anti-Taurus. Kültepe letters mention it as a node where caravans regrouped before the mountain crossing.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mountain town in a steep valley, donkeys resting under pine, snow on the peaks.

stops.name: Kaneš (Kültepe)
stops.note: Destination kārum — the merchant quarter outside the Anatolian city of Kaneš. Tin and textiles unloaded; silver returned south.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lower-town merchant quarter outside a citadel mound, scribes writing on clay, donkeys being unloaded.
